content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5660           322-5951          322-6441            322-6444

August 11, 1981

Patricia Bernard
City Clerk, City of Petaluma
Post and English Streets
Petaluma, CA 94952

Dear Patricia:

This is in response to your letter of August 3, 1981, in which you
asked (1) who is the filing officer for a school board election when
the school district is not wholly within a city or wholly within a
single county, and (2) what is the proper filing schedule for
candidates in a June 2, 1981 election.

In regards to your first question, Government Code Section 84215(d)
states that:

     County elected officers, municipal court judges, candidates
     for such offices, their controlled committees and committees
     formed or existing primarily to support or oppose candidates
     or measures in any number of jurisdictions within one county,
     other than those specified in subdivision (e) [candidates in
     one city], shall file the original and one copy with the clerk
     of the county.

In regards to your second question, Government Code Section 84200(a)
and (b) sets forth the guidelines for filing campaign statements by
candidates in elections held on the first Tuesday after the first Mon-
day in June or November. Page 6 of the Information Manual on Campaign
Disclosure Provisions gives the specific filing schedule for June 2,
and November 3 elections for 1981 and should be followed by all
candidates for such elections.

Government Code Section 84200(c) sets forth the guidelines for filing
campaign statements by candidates in elections held on dates other than
the first Tuesday after the first Monday in June or November. Page 6
of the Information Manual presents the specific schedule which should
be followed by all candidates for elections held on dates other than
June or November 3 in 1981.

If the above advice conflicts with your understanding or interpretation
of any local codes, please feel free to contact me or the staff of the
Legal Division for further advice.

Sincerely,

Lynn Montgomery

CITY OF PETALUMA, California
POST AND ENGLISH STREETS 94952 · TELEPHONE (707) 763-2613

August 3, 1981


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento CA 95814

Attn: Alice Hughes

The City of Petaluma held its biennial City Council and School Board election on
June 2, 1981. In connection with that election, there is some conflict and
question regarding filing Forms 400 series. Please advise the correct procedure
relating to situations listed below:

1. As a charter city, the school board election is governed by our election.
The Education Code recognizes this and even states the entire school district
is considered part of the City when charters cite school board elections in
connection with municipal elections.

Now, who is the filing officer in those circumstances, the City or the County?

But, wait. The situation becomes more awkward.

2. There are different provisions for filing times between June 2 elections and
elections held at any other time.

Page 6 of the 1981 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure states the
1981 filing schedule for June 2 elections to be on activity through June 30
should be reported at a July 31 deadline. That is 28 days after election
the candidates close their books and report that information 59 days after
election.

Page 8 of the same manual says candidates close their books 58 days after
election and make the reporting 65 days after the election.

Now, there is a 30 day difference in the time the candidates are required
to close their books and a 6 day difference in the filing deadline date.

Fair Political Practices Commission
August 3, 1981

-2-

The County Clerk's office has required the school board candidates to file with
the County by the July 31 deadline. I, as Petaluma City Clerk, have requested
all the candidates to file with me giving them the benefit of the 58 day closing
date for accounting purposes and the 65 day deadline date for submitting their
Forms 400 series.

If the County is the filing agency, that is fine with me. Please advise which
Clerk should receive the school board filings for this Charter City, Petaluma.

Now for the filing date discrepancies. It is hoped that all city elections
would be governed by the 58 day closing date after election and 65 day filing
deadline after election rather than that which is listed for June 2 and November 3
elections. Please advise your interpretation of filing regulations on this
matter.

Thank you for your time.

Sincerely yours,

Patricia E. Bernard
City Clerk



cc: County Clerk
     City Attorney
     Acting City Manager

sections.question_synthetic: For a charter city's school board election held in conjunction with a municipal election, should Form 400 series filings be submitted to the City Clerk or County Clerk? Additionally, what are the correct filing deadlines and closing dates for candidates—are they governed by the 28/59-day schedule for June 2 elections or the 58/65-day schedule for other elections?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. OCR quality is poor with significant character corruption throughout the document, making some sections difficult to parse. No conclusion or short answer section is present. The question section is clearly identifiable as the body of the letter from the City Clerk requesting guidance.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from the Petaluma City Clerk to the FPPC dated August 3, 1981, asking for clarification on Form 400 filing procedures and deadlines following a June 2, 1981 charter city election that included school board candidates.
